# memorizer: concise LLM guide

## What memorizer is
memorizer is a memory API for AI agents, with tenant isolation, semantic/hybrid search, reranking, async ingestion, and context/profile composition endpoints.

## Core flow
1. Write memory/documents.
2. Retrieve with search/context/profile.
3. Inject context into LLM prompt.
4. Persist new durable facts.

## Key endpoints
- POST /api/v1/memories
- POST /api/v1/memories/batch
- POST /api/v1/memories/batch/async
- GET /api/v1/memories/search
- POST /api/v1/context
- GET /api/v1/profile
- POST /api/v1/documents
- GET /api/v1/documents
- POST /api/v1/connectors
- POST /api/v1/connectors/{id}/sync

## Retrieval controls
- search_mode: hybrid to combine memories + chunks
- threshold: filter weak matches
- filters: metadata constraints (AND/OR, leaf operators)
- rerank: optional precision boost
- memory_weight/chunk_weight: source balancing

## Auth
Use X-API-Key on every call. Tenant derives from key.

## Operational notes
- API + Worker + DB + Redis compose stack
- Run migrations with Alembic
- Use release/version docs at repo root

For canonical details, read docs/README.md and docs/api-reference.md.
